---
title: "Confidence Intervals - proportions for stratified designs"
---

# Introduction

As documented [here](Confidence%20limits%20using%20the%20Miettinen-Nurminen%20stratified%20method%20as%20de...%20-%20SAS%20Support%20Communities), there is requirement to use CI's for proportions methods other than the wald method, and in certain cases (baseline imbalance of stratification factors), the stratified Miettinen-Nurminen 1985 method is reccomended.

# Packages

There are two available packages in R:

-   [{ratesci}](petelaud/ratesci: Confidence intervals and tests for comparison of rates)
-   [{cicalc}](Stratified Miettinen-Nurminen Confidence Interval for Difference in Proportions — ci_prop_diff_mn_strata • cicalc)

This page is a placeholder whilst further content is being developed.

## {ratesci}

```         
NOTE that: skew = FALSE indicates no skewness correction which gives us the miettinen-nurminen method 

ratesci_out <- scoreci(
    x1 = strat_counts$x1,  n events
    n1 = strat_counts$n1,
    x2 = strat_counts$x2,
    n2 = strat_counts$n2,
    distrib = "bin",
    contrast = "RD",  #risk difference
    level = 0.95, #95% ci
    skew = FALSE, #disable skewness to get MN method
    stratified = TRUE, #combine strata
    weighting = "MH", #MH method as reccomended by MN method
  )
```

## {cicalc}

```         
1=response, 0 = non-responders
Active = 0,  Comparator/Placebo=1.
ci_prop_diff_mn_strata(
  x,
  by,
  strata,
  method = c("score", "summary score"),
  conf.level = 0.95,
  delta = NULL,
  data = NULL
)

dataqc3 <- ci_prop_diff_mn_strata(x = resp, by = trt, strata = strat, data = dataqc2)
```

# References
